title: Account Settings: replace KC console link with sharemember-app /settings
description:
In share-ui, ShaReMemberMenu 'Account Settings' opens accountConsoleUrl (Keycloak console). This should instead navigate to sharemember-app's /settings page. Same for the profile picture edit button in ShareAvatarWithName which opens accountConsoleUrl/profilePicture.

## Changes needed

**share-ui:**
- ShaReMemberMenu.tsx handleAccountClick: replace window.open(accountConsoleUrl) with navigation to share.community/settings
- ShareAvatarWithName.tsx handleEditClick: replace window.open(accountConsoleUrl/profilePicture) with same
- The sharemember-app URL needs to come from config (operator OIDC config or share-ui env var VITE_MEMBER_PORTAL_URL)
- operator-ts config.controller.ts: consider adding memberPortalUrl to OidcConfigDto

**sharemember-app:**
- Already has /settings page (SettingsPage.tsx) with personal info + password + profile picture
- No changes needed on the target side

## Note
This removes the only remaining entry point to Keycloak Account Console from the user-facing UI. 2FA management (separate issue) must be handled in sharemember-app before removing the KC console link entirely.
close_reason: Implemented: ShaReMemberMenu + ShareAvatarWithName now open VITE_SHAREMEMBER_APP_URL/settings. vite-env.d.ts updated with full ImportMetaEnv. Deployed to prod.

title: Add OIDC auth and react-router foundation
description:
Install and configure:
- oidc-client-ts (or @axa-fr/react-oidc) for Keycloak OIDC
- react-router-dom for client-side routing

Routes:
  / — landing page (already exists as PoC)
  /hub — community hub (protected)
  /join — join community via invite code (protected)
  /profile — identity status page (protected)
  /verify — eID verification flow (protected)
  /callback — OIDC redirect callback handler

Auth guard: redirect to Keycloak login if not authenticated.
On login: redirect to /hub.
close_reason: oidc-client-ts + react-router-dom installed; AuthContext + ProtectedRoute + all 5 routes implemented; stubs for hub/join/profile/verify ready for follow-on issues

title: Join community via invite code
description:
Implement /join page.

UI: single invite code input field + [Join] button.

Calls POST /v2/sharemember/me/communities/join with { inviteCode }.

On success:
- Show confirmation: 'You have joined [community name]!'
- Redirect to /hub (community now appears in the list)

On error (invalid code): show inline error message.

Invite codes are distributed offline (person to person) — no discovery UI needed.

title: Profile page — identity status + avatar
description:
Implement /profile page.

Shows:
- Display name (from Keycloak token)
- Avatar (greyscale + dashed border + ? badge if UN-IDENTIFIED; colour + shield badge if IDENTIFIED)
- Identity status banner:
  UN-IDENTIFIED: 'Your identity is not yet verified. Identify yourself to unlock full participation and protect your shaRe presence from impersonation.' + [Verify with eID] CTA
  IDENTIFIED: shield badge + 'Identity verified' + verified date

Reads eidStatus from Keycloak JWT claim or GET /v2/eid/status.

title: Profilbild: Kamera-Aufnahme als Alternative zum Hochladen
description:
Im Profilbild-Editor (SettingsPage) soll neben dem Datei-Upload auch die Gerätekamera nutzbar sein — besonders relevant auf Mobile nach PWA-Installation (SM-e3y).

UX-Flow:
  Zwei Buttons nebeneinander:
  - 'Datei hochladen' (bestehendes Verhalten, input type=file)
  - 'Foto aufnehmen' (öffnet Kamera via input type=file accept=image/* capture=environment)

Technisch:
  HTML input mit capture='environment' öffnet auf Mobile direkt die Rückkamera.
  Kein getUserMedia/MediaStream nötig — der bestehende Canvas-Editor
  (drag/zoom, PNG-Export) funktioniert identisch für beide Quellen.
  Auf Desktop wo keine Kamera verfügbar ist: Button ausblenden oder
  Browser zeigt Datei-Dialog (graceful fallback).

Abhängigkeit: profitiert von SM-e3y (PWA), ist aber kein harter Blocker.
Status: offen, noch nicht angehen.

title: Personal Info & Passwort in sharemember-app
description:
Persönliche Daten direkt in sharemember-app verwalten, ohne Umweg über die KC-Account-Page.

## Scope

### Personal Info (Name, E-Mail)
- GET /realms/{realm}/account — Felder vorladen in Formular
- POST /realms/{realm}/account — Änderungen speichern
- Felder: firstName, lastName, email

### Passwort ändern
- PUT /realms/{realm}/account/credentials/password
- Body: { currentPassword, newPassword, confirmation }

### Aktive Sessions
- GET /realms/{realm}/account/sessions — Liste aller Geräte/Sessions
- DELETE /realms/{realm}/account/sessions/{id} — einzelne Session abmelden
- DELETE /realms/{realm}/account/sessions — alle abmelden (außer aktueller)

## Technisches
- Bearer-Token: user.accessToken aus AuthContext
- Base URL: VITE_SSO_URL + /realms/ + VITE_KEYCLOAK_REALM + /account
- Prüfen ob prod-sharemember-app Client 'account' als Audience hat (ggf. KC-Config nötig)
- Neue Sektion auf ProfilePage oder eigene /settings Route

## Kein Backend nötig — alles direkt gegen KC Account REST API
close_reason: Settings page implemented: Personal Info + Change Password via KC Account REST API. /settings route live at share.community.

title: eID verification page — /verify flow
description:
Implement /verify page.

Step-by-step guidance UI explaining what eID is and what the user needs (German Personalausweis + AusweisApp).

Flow:
1. User clicks [Start eID verification]
2. Calls GET /v2/eid/start → receives redirect URL to Verimi/eID server
3. Redirect user to eID server
4. After eID flow, user is redirected back to /verify?status=...
5. On success: POST /v2/eid/callback (or operator-ts handles callback directly)
6. Redirect to /profile — now showing IDENTIFIED status

Error states: user cancelled, eID already used by another account (duplicate).

title: sharemember-app MVP — identity hub + community portal
description:
MVP implementation of the sharemember-app React frontend.

Repo: https://github.com/shaRe-Community/sharemember-app
Spec: ../share-platform/shaRe-eid-integration-spec.md

The app is the front door to the entire shaRe ecosystem:
- Login once at share.community → see all your communities → enter via SSO
- Join a community via invite code
- Manage your IDENTIFIED / UN-IDENTIFIED status
- Trigger eID verification flow

Stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite 7 + oidc-client-ts + react-router

title: Investigate empty share_member_id for existing users
description:
share_member_id is a KC user attribute (system key) that stores the Neo4j ShareMember node ID, set by operator-ts when a shareholder first joins a community. It should NOT be user-editable and should not appear in settings UI.

## Problem
tom.blume (KC id: 29ce29c8-bf39-4495-8353-e67f4cdba382) has empty share_member_id attribute despite being an active user. eid_status is also unset (defaults to 'un_identified' via KC plugin claim mapper).

## Investigation needed
1. Check if ShareMember node exists in Neo4j for this user:
   MATCH (sm:ShareMember {keycloakId: '29ce29c8-bf39-4495-8353-e67f4cdba382'}) RETURN sm
2. Check operator-ts ShareMember creation code — does it write share_member_id back to KC?
   src/shareholders/shareholders.service.ts or similar
3. If ShareMember node exists but KC attribute is missing → backfill script needed
4. If KC attribute write is missing from operator-ts → fix the write path

## Note on eid_status
Also unset for tom.blume — this is expected for un-identified users (the JWT claim mapper defaults to 'un_identified' when attribute is absent, per KC plugin logic). No action needed here unless the claim mapper is NOT providing the default.

## Decision
share_member_id is purely a system foreign key. It should:
- NEVER be shown in user-facing settings
- NEVER be editable by users
- Be populated automatically by operator-ts on shareholder creation
- Potentially be shown as read-only in a developer/debug panel only
close_reason: Backfilled: created ShareMember nodes in Neo4j for Tom Blume + Manfred Schon, wrote share_member_id + eid_status to KC. Root cause: User Profile SPI silently dropped attributes before we added declarations. KC user cache cleared.

title: Add MUI to sharemember-app
description:
Replace custom CSS with Material UI (MUI) for component consistency with share-ui.

## Motivation
sharemember-app currently uses hand-rolled CSS components. share-ui uses MUI throughout. Adding MUI would:
- Give sharemember-app the same component quality as share-ui (Autocomplete, Menu, Dialog, etc.)
- Allow reuse of MUI's LanguageSwitcher pattern (IconButton + Menu + MenuItem + CheckIcon) instead of the custom native dropdown
- Standardise theming (dark palette, primary red #FF3B2F) across both apps

## Scope
- npm install @mui/material @emotion/react @emotion/styled @mui/icons-material
- Create MUI theme matching existing dark navy palette (background #031633 / #18203a, primary #FF3B2F)
- Replace custom CSS components file-by-file: AppShell header, CommunityCard grid, JoinPage form, ProfilePage card, VerifyPage card
- Replace LanguageSwitcher native dropdown with MUI IconButton + Menu pattern (same as share-ui)
- Keep i18n setup unchanged

## Notes
- Do NOT do this mid-feature — clean break, one PR
- share-ui MUI theme is at src/theme.ts (reference for colour tokens)
- LanguageSwitcher in share-ui (src/components/LanguageSwitcher.tsx) can be copied almost verbatim

title: PWA: sharemember-app installierbar machen
description:
sharemember-app als Progressive Web App (PWA) ausliefern, sodass sie auf iOS und Android auf den Homescreen installiert werden kann.

Motivation:
- Peer Vouching passiert im realen Kontext (Treffen, Gemeindeversammlung) → Handy ist dabei, Laptop nicht
- QR-Code-Scan für Peer Vouching braucht Kamera-Zugriff → Mobile-native
- Push-Notifications für Vouch-Anfragen sind auf Mobile zuverlässiger als Browser-Push
- Kein App Store Overhead — gleiche Codebasis, sofortige Updates

Technischer Ansatz: vite-plugin-pwa
- Manifest.json (Name, Icons, theme_color, display: standalone)
- Service Worker mit Workbox (Precaching der App-Shell)
- Icons: 192×192 und 512×512 (shaRe-Logo)
- iOS: apple-touch-icon, apple-mobile-web-app-capable meta tags

Schritte:
1. vite-plugin-pwa installieren
2. vite.config.ts konfigurieren (manifest + workbox)
3. Icons in verschiedenen Größen erstellen
4. In main.tsx Service Worker registrieren
5. Auf iOS + Android testen (Add to Homescreen)

Nicht im Scope:
- Native App (React Native / Capacitor) — PWA reicht für MVP
- Offline-Funktionalität über App-Shell hinaus
- Background Sync

Abhängigkeit: Peer Vouching UX profitiert davon, ist aber kein Blocker.

title: 2FA management in sharemember-app settings
description:
Currently 2FA (TOTP) is only accessible via the Keycloak account console. After SM-05x removes that link, users need a way to manage 2FA in sharemember-app.

## What to build

New section in SettingsPage.tsx: 'Two-Factor Authentication'

### Status display
- GET /realms/{realm}/account/credentials (with user.accessToken)
- Detect if a TOTP credential exists (type='otp')
- Show: enabled (with authenticator app icon) or disabled

### Enable 2FA
- Redirect to Keycloak's TOTP setup action URL:
  {issuer}/login-actions/required-action?execution=CONFIGURE_TOTP&client_id={clientId}
- After completion, KC redirects back → user sees updated status
- This is pragmatic: TOTP QR generation + verification belongs in KC, we just trigger the flow

### Disable 2FA
- Button shown only when TOTP credential exists
- DELETE /realms/{realm}/account/credentials/{credentialId} (with accessToken)
- Confirm dialog before deletion
- Refresh credential list after

### i18n keys needed (en + de)
- settings.two_factor_title
- settings.two_factor_enabled / two_factor_disabled
- settings.two_factor_enable / two_factor_disable
- settings.two_factor_confirm_disable
- settings.two_factor_setup_redirect (note that setup opens external page)

## KC Account REST API reference
- GET /realms/{realm}/account/credentials → array of {id, type, userLabel, ...}
- DELETE /realms/{realm}/account/credentials/{id}
- TOTP setup page: {issuer}/login-actions/required-action?execution=CONFIGURE_TOTP
close_reason: Implemented 2FA section in SettingsPage: status display, enable (KC account console redirect), disable (DELETE credential API). Inline fetch pattern, correct error keys, both locales. Deployed.

title: Community hub page — list + enter communities
description:
Implement /hub page.

Fetches GET /v2/sharemember/me/communities from operator-ts and renders community cards.

Each card shows:
- Community name + intent
- logoUrl (image or placeholder)
- memberCount
- purposeRoundtable name
- [Enter →] button → opens community frontendUrl (direct redirect, SSO via Keycloak)

Empty state: 'You are not a member of any community yet.' + [Join a community] CTA.

UN-IDENTIFIED banner at top if eidStatus === 'un_identified':
  'Your identity is not yet verified. [Verify with eID →]'
